title: Preallocate before iterating
description: Size a result vector up front instead of growing it with c() or append inside a loop.
body: Use when a loop is genuinely required and accumulates results. Do not grow a vector with c(x, new) or append inside the loop — each step copies the whole structure, turning the work quadratic. Actions: allocate the result to its final length (vector(mode, n) or numeric(n)), assign by index, or prefer vapply/map which preallocate internally. Validation signals include a benchmark that stays linear as n grows. Risk boundary: preallocating with the wrong mode silently coerces values — confirm the result type matches the assigned values.
